title: SIGNAL_STUDIO_MAX_YAML_SIZE_KB is read without validation; 0 or negative silently breaks config analysis
summary: In backend/internal/api/handlers.go handleAnalyzeConfig parses SIGNAL_STUDIO_MAX_YAML_SIZE_KB with strconv.Atoi and assigns it directly to maxSize with no lower-bound check. It then calls io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(r.Body, int64(maxSize)*1024)). A value of 0 (or negative) makes LimitReader return EOF immediately, so the body is read as empty and every /api/config/analyze request fails with a confusing YAML parse error rather than a clear configuration error. This is inconsistent with SIGNAL_STUDIO_SCRAPE_INTERVAL_SECONDS in cmd/server/serve.go, which validates the parsed value (n>=5 && n<=30) before using it.
why_good_first_issue: Single-file, ~3-line fix mirroring the existing validation pattern already in serve.go. No architectural change, no ADR needed, easy to add a unit test (handlers_test.go already exists). Clear correct-vs-incorrect behaviour.
affected_files: backend/internal/api/handlers.go (lines 42-49); pattern reference: backend/cmd/server/serve.go (lines 38-42); doc: backend/internal/serverconfig/env.go (line 18)
repro_steps: 1) Clone repo. 2) Read backend/internal/api/handlers.go lines 42-49: maxSize := 256; if v := os.Getenv("SIGNAL_STUDIO_MAX_YAML_SIZE_KB"); v != "" { if n, err := strconv.Atoi(v); err == nil { maxSize = n } }; body, err := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(r.Body, int64(maxSize)*1024)). No check that n>0. 3) Compare to backend/cmd/server/serve.go lines 38-42 which DOES guard: if n, err := strconv.Atoi(v); err == nil && n >= 5 && n <= 30 { ... }. 4) Go stdlib semantics: io.LimitReader(r, 0) yields a reader that returns (0, io.EOF) immediately; io.ReadAll returns empty []byte; config.Parse(empty) then errors. Go toolchain not installed on VM (command -v go -> not found) so verified by inspection of source + documented stdlib behaviour.
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection: handlers.go:42-49 assigns maxSize=n with no >0 guard, unlike serve.go:38-42 (n>=5 && n<=30). grep over backend/**/*.go shows no test exercises maxSize<=0 (only refs are env.go:18, handlers.go:42-49). With SIGNAL_STUDIO_MAX_YAML_SIZE_KB=0 or a negative value, io.LimitReader limit becomes 0/negative => empty read => config.Parse fails for every analyze request. Go absent on VM so runtime not executed; logic is unambiguous.
fix_hint: Add a lower-bound guard before assigning, e.g. if n, err := strconv.Atoi(v); err == nil && n > 0 { maxSize = n } (optionally cap with an upper bound), matching serve.go's pattern. Add a handlers_test.go case asserting maxSize falls back to default when the env var is 0/negative/non-numeric.
notes: Repo language: Go (gh api .language=Go). 0 open issues, so self-identified. Go 1.25 per backend/go.mod; backend coverage must stay >=80% per the repo's coverage policy, so include a unit test. Inspection-tier only because Go is not installed on VM and must-not-install constraint applies.

title: Dockerfile/README Quick Start do not expose or publish OTLP tap ports (5317/5318) although the tap is enabled by default
summary: The OTLP sampling tap is a headline feature and is 'Enabled by default' per README. It listens on :5317 (gRPC) and :5318 (HTTP) (defaults in router.go and tap_handler.go). However the Dockerfile only declares EXPOSE 8080 and the README Quick Start only runs docker run -p 8080:8080. A user following Quick Start cannot reach the tap endpoints because the container does not expose/publish 5317/5318, so pointing a Collector at the tap fails with connection refused.
why_good_first_issue: Pure docs/Dockerfile change: add EXPOSE 5317 5318 to the Dockerfile and update the Quick Start docker run example to publish those ports (-p 5317:5317 -p 5318:5318). No code logic involved, trivially verifiable against existing config values.
affected_files: Dockerfile (EXPOSE 8080 only); README.md (Quick Start docker run -p 8080:8080; Features/OTLP Sampling Tap section says 'Enabled by default'); defaults: backend/internal/api/router.go (lines 22-30), backend/internal/api/tap_handler.go (defaultGRPCAddr/defaultHTTPAddr)
repro_steps: 1) README.md Quick Start: docker build -t signal-studio . ; docker run -p 8080:8080 signal-studio. 2) README.md states the OTLP Sampling Tap is 'Enabled by default; disable with SIGNAL_STUDIO_TAP_DISABLED=true'. 3) README config table lists SIGNAL_STUDIO_TAP_GRPC_ADDR=:5317 and SIGNAL_STUDIO_TAP_HTTP_ADDR=:5318. 4) Dockerfile declares only EXPOSE 8080 (no 5317/5318) and ENTRYPOINT runs the server which, with tap enabled, listens on those ports. 5) Therefore a container started per Quick Start does not publish 5317/5318 and the tap is unreachable from the host/Collector.
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection: Dockerfile has a single 'EXPOSE 8080' line and no mention of 5317/5318; grep '5317|5318' across *.md/Dockerfile returns only README.md:56-57 (the env var table) and ADR docs, never the Dockerfile or the Quick Start docker run command. README explicitly says the tap is enabled by default. Net effect: default-on feature is unreachable when following the documented Quick Start.
fix_hint: Add 'EXPOSE 5317 5318' to the Dockerfile and update the README Quick Start docker run example to '-p 8080:8080 -p 5317:5317 -p 5318:5318' (or note that the tap ports must be published). Optionally mention SIGNAL_STUDIO_TAP_DISABLED for users who do not want the tap.
notes: Repo language: Go. 0 open issues; self-identified. Inspection-tier (no deploy; Docker build not run on the constrained VM and Go not installed). Low risk, doc-only.
